questions.stem: This labeled diagram depicts the layered structure of the epidermis. Why are dermatophyte infections confined to the superficial region shown here rather than invading deeper tissue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dermatophytes require keratin and cannot survive in the living, keratin-poor deeper tissue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dermatophytes are obligate intracellular organisms of basal cells

choices.label: C
choices.text: Blood vessels in the dermis physically wall off the fungus

choices.label: D
choices.text: Dermatophytes are killed instantly by melanocyte pigment

choices.label: E
choices.text: The dermis contains antifungal proteins that block invasion

explanation.summary: Dermatophytes feed on keratin found only in the stratum corneum, hair, and nails, so they remain limited to keratinized superficial layers and rarely cause deep or systemic disease.
explanation.whyCorrect: Dermatophytes feed on keratin found only in the stratum corneum, hair, and nails, so they remain limited to keratinized superficial layers and rarely cause deep or systemic disease.

questions.stem: Under this ultraviolet lamp the infected area fluoresces. What is the correct interpretation of a positive and a negative result?

choices.label: B
choices.text: A negative test definitively excludes fungal infection

choices.label: C
choices.text: Some dermatophytes fluoresce; a negative test does NOT rule out infection

choices.label: D
choices.text: A positive test confirms bacterial infection

choices.label: E
choices.text: All dermatophytes fluoresce, so the test is fully diagnostic

explanation.summary: A Wood's lamp causes some dermatophytes (e.g., Microsporum canis) to fluoresce, but many do not, so a negative test cannot exclude a fungal infection.
explanation.whyCorrect: A Wood's lamp causes some dermatophytes (e.g., Microsporum canis) to fluoresce, but many do not, so a negative test cannot exclude a fungal infection.

questions.stem: This microscopy shows smooth, club/beaver-tail-shaped macroconidia in clusters with no microconidia. Which genus is this?

explanation.summary: Epidermophyton produces smooth, club-shaped macroconidia in clusters and characteristically lacks microconidia.
explanation.whyCorrect: Epidermophyton produces smooth, club-shaped macroconidia in clusters and characteristically lacks microconidia.

questions.stem: This microscopy shows cigar-shaped macroconidia with abundant microconidia. Which dermatophyte genus does this represent?

explanation.summary: Trichophyton is distinguished by cigar-shaped macroconidia, numerous microconidia, and sometimes coiled or spiraled hyphae.
explanation.whyCorrect: Trichophyton is distinguished by cigar-shaped macroconidia, numerous microconidia, and sometimes coiled or spiraled hyphae.

questions.stem: This microscopy shows rough-walled, spindle-shaped macroconidia with microconidia present. Which genus is identified?

explanation.summary: Microsporum is identified by rough-walled, spindle-shaped macroconidia, in contrast to the smooth macroconidia of Epidermophyton.
explanation.whyCorrect: Microsporum is identified by rough-walled, spindle-shaped macroconidia, in contrast to the smooth macroconidia of Epidermophyton.

questions.stem: This fungal colony is growing on the medium of choice for isolating dermatophytes. What medium is this and why is it selective?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Chocolate agar, enriched for fastidious bacteria

choices.label: C
choices.text: Sabouraud dextrose agar, selective due to its low pH

choices.label: D
choices.text: MacConkey agar, selective for gram-negative rods

choices.label: E
choices.text: Blood agar, selective for hemolytic organisms

explanation.summary: Sabouraud dextrose agar has a low pH (~5.6) that inhibits most bacterial growth, making it selective for fungi.
explanation.whyCorrect: Sabouraud dextrose agar has a low pH (~5.6) that inhibits most bacterial growth, making it selective for fungi.

questions.stem: This KOH preparation reveals clustered round yeast cells together with short hyphal elements. What is the classic descriptive term for this appearance?

explanation.summary: Malassezia in a KOH prep shows clustered yeast cells (meatballs) with short hyphae (spaghetti), the classic 'spaghetti and meatballs' appearance.
explanation.whyCorrect: Malassezia in a KOH prep shows clustered yeast cells (meatballs) with short hyphae (spaghetti), the classic 'spaghetti and meatballs' appearance.

questions.stem: This tissue biopsy shows a central yeast surrounded by radiating, star-shaped eosinophilic material within a giant cell. What is this structure called?

explanation.summary: Asteroid bodies are star-shaped structures surrounding the yeast within giant cells of granulomas, seen in sporotrichosis, especially disseminated disease.
explanation.whyCorrect: Asteroid bodies are star-shaped structures surrounding the yeast within giant cells of granulomas, seen in sporotrichosis, especially disseminated disease.

questions.stem: This annular skin lesion with a raised scaly advancing border and central clearing is most consistent with which condition?

explanation.summary: The classic ring shape with an active scaly border and central clearing is produced as the dermatophyte spreads outward while the immune response clears the center.
explanation.whyCorrect: The classic ring shape with an active scaly border and central clearing is produced as the dermatophyte spreads outward while the immune response clears the center.

questions.stem: This child's scalp shows a patch of hair loss with scaling and broken-off hairs. What is the most likely diagnosis?

explanation.summary: Scaly patches with patchy alopecia and broken hairs (black dots) in a child are characteristic of dermatophyte scalp infection, tinea capitis.
explanation.whyCorrect: Scaly patches with patchy alopecia and broken hairs (black dots) in a child are characteristic of dermatophyte scalp infection, tinea capitis.

questions.stem: This infant scalp demonstrates patchy hair loss from a dermatophyte infection. Which genera most commonly cause this presentation?

explanation.summary: Tinea capitis is most frequently caused by Trichophyton and Microsporum species; Epidermophyton does not infect hair.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tinea capitis is most frequently caused by Trichophyton and Microsporum species; Epidermophyton does not infect hair.

questions.stem: This inflamed, crusted eruption over a man's beard area is most consistent with which infection?

explanation.summary: Tinea barbae is a dermatophyte infection of the beard region, typically acquired from animals and presenting with itchy, red, crusting lesions and hair loss.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tinea barbae is a dermatophyte infection of the beard region, typically acquired from animals and presenting with itchy, red, crusting lesions and hair loss.

questions.stem: This farm animal shows circular patches of hair loss with crusting from a dermatophyte. Why is recognizing this relevant to human infection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It proves the infection is non-contagious to humans

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is a zoonotic reservoir that can transmit dermatophytes to humans

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is the definitive sexual host of the fungus

choices.label: E
choices.text: It shows the fungus is dimorphic only in cattle

explanation.summary: Livestock such as cattle harbor zoophilic dermatophytes (e.g., Trichophyton verrucosum) that can be transmitted to farmers and animal workers.
explanation.whyCorrect: Livestock such as cattle harbor zoophilic dermatophytes (e.g., Trichophyton verrucosum) that can be transmitted to farmers and animal workers.

questions.stem: This trunk lesion shows an expanding red patch with central clearing. Which organism is a particularly common cause among pet owners?

explanation.summary: Microsporum canis is a zoophilic dermatophyte commonly transmitted from cats and dogs, making it a frequent cause of tinea corporis in pet owners.
explanation.whyCorrect: Microsporum canis is a zoophilic dermatophyte commonly transmitted from cats and dogs, making it a frequent cause of tinea corporis in pet owners.

questions.stem: This erythematous scaly eruption in the groin fold that spares the scrotum is most consistent with which condition?

explanation.summary: Tinea cruris (jock itch) affects the groin and inner thighs with an advancing scaly border and characteristically spares the scrotum, unlike candidal infection.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tinea cruris (jock itch) affects the groin and inner thighs with an advancing scaly border and characteristically spares the scrotum, unlike candidal infection.

questions.stem: This maceration and scaling between the toes represents which form of tinea pedis?

explanation.summary: The interdigital form is the most common presentation of athlete's foot, with macerated, scaly skin predominantly between the toes.
explanation.whyCorrect: The interdigital form is the most common presentation of athlete's foot, with macerated, scaly skin predominantly between the toes.

questions.stem: This foot shows tense blisters and erosions from a dermatophyte infection. Which form of tinea pedis is this?

explanation.summary: The vesiculobullous form presents with painful, itchy blisters on the soles that are often intensely red and swollen.
explanation.whyCorrect: The vesiculobullous form presents with painful, itchy blisters on the soles that are often intensely red and swollen.

questions.stem: These thickened, discolored, crumbling nails are most consistent with which condition?

explanation.summary: Tinea unguium (onychomycosis) is a dermatophyte nail infection causing thick, discolored, brittle nails that are often resistant to topical therapy.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tinea unguium (onychomycosis) is a dermatophyte nail infection causing thick, discolored, brittle nails that are often resistant to topical therapy.

questions.stem: This inflamed, macerated eruption arising where two skin surfaces rub together is best described as?

explanation.summary: Intertrigo is inflammation of skin folds caused by friction, moisture, and irritation, and it can predispose to secondary bacterial and fungal infection.
explanation.whyCorrect: Intertrigo is inflammation of skin folds caused by friction, moisture, and irritation, and it can predispose to secondary bacterial and fungal infection.

questions.stem: This trunk shows well-demarcated patches of altered pigmentation with fine scale that became noticeable after tanning. What is the diagnosis?

explanation.summary: Malassezia overgrowth causes hypo- or hyperpigmented, finely scaly patches on the trunk that become obvious after sun exposure tans the surrounding skin.
explanation.whyCorrect: Malassezia overgrowth causes hypo- or hyperpigmented, finely scaly patches on the trunk that become obvious after sun exposure tans the surrounding skin.

questions.stem: This limb shows an ulcerating nodule with additional nodules tracking proximally along the lymphatics. Which infection produces this pattern?

explanation.summary: Sporothrix schenckii inoculated through the skin produces a papule that ulcerates and spreads along lymphatic channels as a chain of nodules ('chain of pearls').
explanation.whyCorrect: Sporothrix schenckii inoculated through the skin produces a papule that ulcerates and spreads along lymphatic channels as a chain of nodules ('chain of pearls').

questions.stem: This household object is highlighted as a common source of dermatophyte spread. What role does it play in transmission?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is a reservoir where the fungus completes sexual reproduction

choices.label: B
choices.text: It acts as a fomite that harbors and transfers infectious arthrospores

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is a biological vector that amplifies the fungus

choices.label: D
choices.text: It supplies the keratinase enzyme needed for infection

choices.label: E
choices.text: It is a definitive host required for the fungal life cycle

explanation.summary: Brushes, combs, and hats are inanimate fomites that carry shed skin and hair laden with dermatophyte arthrospores, allowing indirect person-to-person transmission.
explanation.whyCorrect: Brushes, combs, and hats are inanimate fomites that carry shed skin and hair laden with dermatophyte arthrospores, allowing indirect person-to-person transmission.
